name: Chile

visa_free.nationality: All nationalities NOT listed in Decreto Supremo N°359 (2024)
visa_free.notes: Under Article 27 of Ley N°21.325 de Migración y Extranjería, nationals of countries NOT enumerated in DS 359 enter Chile visa-free as holders of 'Permiso de Permanencia Transitoria' for up to 90 days (extendable once for 90 days). The decree published 17 Sep 2025 lists 104 nationalities that DO require prior authorization. All others are implicitly visa-free. Exceptions exist for holders of agreements/treaties per Article 2 of DS 359. Visitors must demonstrate a minimum of USD 46/day of funds for the duration of their stay (Resolución Exenta Nº 3.464 de 2023).

visa_policy.notes: Chile's visa policy is governed by Ley N°21.325 de Migración y Extranjería (Art. 27). The default is visa-free entry (Permiso de Permanencia Transitoria) for up to 90 days, extendable once for 90 more days. Decreto Supremo N°359 (signed 8 Nov 2024, published 17 Sep 2025) lists 104 nationalities that require prior Chilean consulate authorization or visa to obtain this permit. Nationals on the DS-359 list who hold valid agreements/treaties with Chile may be exempted per Article 2 of that decree. Australia was explicitly removed from the visa-required list in DS 359 due to international reciprocity. Visitors must demonstrate minimum funds of USD 46/day (Resolución Exenta Nº 3.464 de 2023). The decree structure means all nationalities NOT on the 104-country list are effectively visa-free.

credential.subtype: any valid visa (including Green Card/permanent residence), except type C (transit)

conditional_access.conditions: Indian citizens holding a valid US visa of any type (including Green Card / permanent residence) - except type C (transit) - with at least 6 months remaining validity at time of arrival in Chile are exempt from obtaining a Chilean visa. Entry as tourist, no paid employment. Verbatim: 'Los/as ciudadanos/as indios/as, titulares de un visado estadounidense de cualquier tipo (incluyendo Green Card) -exceptuando el tipo C (tránsito)- con al menos seis meses de validez en el momento de la llegada a Chile, estarán exentos de obtener visa'.

credential.subtype: any valid visa except type C (transit)

conditional_access.conditions: Dominican nationals holding a valid US visa of any type - except type C (transit) - with at least 6 months remaining validity at time of travel may enter Chile as a tourist without a Chilean visa. Verbatim: 'Los nacionales dominicanos que tengan visa de los Estados Unidos de América, de cualquier tipo a excepción del visado tipo C (de tránsito), con una vigencia restante de al menos 6 meses al momento de viaje, no necesitan visa para ingresar a Chile en calidad de Turista.'

credential.subtype: any valid visa (including Green Card), except type C (transit)

conditional_access.conditions: Chinese nationals holding any valid US visa type (including Green Card/permanent residence) - except type C - with at least 6 months remaining validity may enter Chile visa-free as a tourist for up to 90 days. No paid employment permitted. Source: Sección Consular de Chile en Beijing (chile.gob.cl/beijing).

credential.subtype: any valid visa (including permanent residence), except type C (transit)

conditional_access.conditions: Chinese nationals holding any valid Canadian visa type (including permanent residence) - except type C - with at least 6 months remaining validity may enter Chile visa-free as a tourist for up to 90 days. No paid employment permitted. Source: Sección Consular de Chile en Beijing (chile.gob.cl/beijing).

Chile has approximately 73 bilateral visa exemption agreements for holders of diplomatic, official, and/or service passports. Under these agreements, nationals of the partner country holding the specified passport type may enter Chile visa-free for up to 90 days (tourism, no paid employment). Officials posted to a diplomatic mission or consular office may remain for the duration of their assignment. Confirmed individual agreements include: Switzerland (diplomatic/official/service passports, signed 19 Jan 2024, ratified by Chilean Senate 7 Mar 2025); Oman (diplomatic/special/service/official passports, signed 22 Mar 2022); Morocco (diplomatic/official/special/service passports, signed 20 Sep 2016); South Africa (diplomatic/official passports, signed 1 Sep 2016); Azerbaijan (diplomatic/service/official passports, signed 13 Sep 2018); Bolivia (diplomatic/official passports, signed 25 Apr 2025, in force Mar 2026). Full authoritative list in MINREL VIDO Manual (May 2025 edition) - image-based PDF, not machine-readable.

cbi.notes: Chile does not offer a citizenship-by-investment program. Citizenship is obtained only through naturalization after accumulating 5 years of legal residence (combining temporary and permanent residency periods). No expedited citizenship pathway for investors exists under current law.

programs.name: Residencia Temporal - Inversores y Personal Relacionado (Investor Temporary Residency)
programs.notes: Requires investment of USD 500,000 or equivalent in productive goods or services in the planning or execution phase (residential real estate for personal use does not qualify). Alternatively available to legal representatives, directors and senior management of foreign companies, or specialized technical/management personnel of Chilean companies where a qualifying foreign investor controls at least 10% voting rights. Requires a sponsorship letter from INVESTCHILE (Chile Foreign Investment Agency) per Interior Ministry Supreme Decree No. 177 of 2022 (Arts. 62-64). Must apply from outside Chile via SERMIG digital portal. Issued for maximum 2 years (renewable with tax folder and investment proof). After 2 years temporal residency, holders may qualify for Residencia Definitiva (permanent residency). After 5 total years of legal residence, eligible for citizenship by naturalization. USD 500,000 threshold re-confirmed on SERMIG investor page (June 2026 verification).

programs.name: Residencia Temporal - Reciprocidad Internacional MERCOSUR
programs.notes: Available to nationals of Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay under the Mercosur Residence Agreement (signed 6 Dec 2002, in force 28 Jul 2009). Bolivia-Chile bilateral agreement eliminates application fees for Bolivian nationals. Valid for up to 2 years. Holders may engage in lawful remunerated activities. Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru ratified the Mercosur Residence Agreement as of January 2024 and may also be covered.

programs.name: Residencia Temporal - Rentistas y Pensionados (Retired Persons and Income Earners)
programs.notes: Available to foreign nationals who can demonstrate sufficient regular income (rental income, dividends, salaries, pensions). Specific minimum income thresholds are not published verbatim on the official SERMIG page; amounts cited by third parties (~USD 1,500-2,000/month) could not be verified on the official site. Issued for maximum 2 years renewable.

programs.name: Residencia Definitiva (Permanent Residency)
programs.notes: Available after 24 months of Residencia Temporal (standard), or 12 months if the applicant has family ties with Chilean nationals/permanent residents, prior official missions in Chile, stable income/pensions, active business investments, or qualifies under international agreements. Permit is valid for life; ID card renewed every 5 years. Absence from Chile for more than 2 continuous years voids the permit. Both timelines re-confirmed on SERMIG Residencia Definitiva page (June 2026 verification).

programs.name: Working Holiday Agreement - Australia
programs.eligibility: Australian nationals aged up to 31 years
programs.notes: Bilateral Working Holiday Agreement between Chile and Australia. Allows young Australian nationals to live and work in Chile temporarily. Age limit up to 31 years. Australia was also removed from the DS 359 visa-required list (effective Sep 2025) due to international reciprocity, meaning Australians now also enter visa-free as tourists for up to 90 days.

programs.name: Working Holiday Agreement - New Zealand
programs.eligibility: New Zealand nationals aged up to 30 years
programs.notes: Bilateral Working Holiday Agreement between Chile and New Zealand. Allows young New Zealand nationals to live and work in Chile temporarily. Age limit up to 30 years.

agreements: Mercosur Residence Agreement (associate member - Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, Uruguay; and Colombia, Ecuador, Peru from Jan 2024)

outbound_official.notes: Chile's official outbound visa information is published by the Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores through its consular portal (consulado.gob.cl). There is no single consolidated list page; the hub page at /chilenas-os-que-viajan-fuera-de-chile highlights recent electronic-authorization changes, and per-country detail pages at /quiero-viajar/info-para-viajar-fuera-de-chile/visas/[country-slug] each state '¿Requiere Visa de Turismo? SI/NO'. Countries confirmed visa-free (NO) from individual pages verified in June 2026: Argentina, Colombia, Costa Rica, Japan, United Kingdom (no prior consular visa but UK ETA required since 2024), Netherlands/Spain/Italy/Greece/Iceland (Schengen - no prior visa, 90 days per 180). Countries confirmed visa-required (SI): USA (ESTA Visa Waiver available), Australia (eVisitor/ETA available). Electronic authorizations highlighted on hub page: UK (ETA), Canada (eTA, entry itself is NO-visa), New Zealand (NZeTA, entry itself appears conditional), South Korea (K-ETA), USA (ESTA under Visa Waiver Program), Israel (ETA-IL effective Jan 2025). e-Visa required: Ethiopia, Indonesia (digital VOA), Cuba (electronic tourist visa), Mauritania (electronic visa). The full alphabetical country index is available only via the per-country dropdown selector on the site (approx. 195 destinations covered).

visa_on_arrival.name: Indonesia
visa_on_arrival.note: Digital visa on arrival via All Indonesia system (effective Oct 2025)

visa_on_arrival.name: Tanzania (Zanzibar)
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival available; travel insurance mandatory for stays up to 91 days

e_visa.name: Australia
e_visa.note: Visa required (SI); eVisitor / ETA available online

eta.name: United Kingdom
eta.note: No prior consular visa (NO) but UK Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) required for stays under 90 days (effective 2024)

eta.name: Canada
eta.note: No prior consular visa (NO*) but eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization) required

eta.name: United States
eta.note: Visa required (SI) but ESTA authorization available under US Visa Waiver Program (effective March 2014; Chile is the only Latin American country in VWP); stays up to 90 days

visa_types.name: Permiso de Permanencia Transitoria - Turismo
visa_types.purpose: Grants temporary entry to Chile for tourism, sports, or similar non-remunerative purposes with no intention of settling. Holders may not engage in paid work.
visa_types.notes: Extendable once for an additional 90 days (maximum 180 days total). Holders must demonstrate minimum USD 46/day of funds (Resolución Exenta Nº 3.464 de 2023). Nationals of 104 countries (listed in Decreto Supremo N°359) require prior consulate authorization. Cannot convert to residency permit except via specific family/humanitarian routes.

visa_types.name: Permiso de Permanencia Transitoria - Tránsito
visa_types.purpose: Permits international passenger and cargo transport crew members and others in transit to stay in Chile temporarily.
visa_types.notes: Covers crew members of international passenger and cargo transport and individuals covered by international treaties. Part of the broader Permanencia Transitoria framework under Ley N°21.325.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Actividades Remuneradas (Remunerated Activities)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to reside in Chile and engage in lawful paid employment under an employment contract, service contract, or formal job offer.
visa_types.notes: Standard grant is up to 2 years, extendable for two successive 2-year periods. Job-offer pathway grants initial 90 days then 1-year extension upon presenting employment contract within 45 days of entry. Application must be submitted from outside Chile via SERMIG digital portal. All foreign documents require apostille or legalization.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Trabajadores de Temporada (Seasonal Workers)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to reside in Chile for seasonal employment directly related to the employer's main line of business.
visa_types.notes: Permit valid for up to 5 years (extendable for 2 more years) but actual stay limited to no more than 6 months per calendar year. Requires employment contract signed before a Chilean notary. Applicants have 45 days after entry to present employment contract. Nationals of bilateral-agreement countries may apply with a formal job offer instead.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Estudiantes (Students)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals enrolled in state-recognized Chilean educational institutions to reside in Chile for the duration of their studies.
visa_types.notes: Holders may engage in lawful paid activities for up to 30 hours per week without additional authorization. Requires proof of enrollment and financial means. Extension requires proof of continued enrollment and financial capacity. Application must be made from outside Chile via SERMIG digital portal.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Inversores y Personal Relacionado (Investors and Related Personnel)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign investors making a minimum USD 500,000 investment in productive goods or services in Chile, and their key personnel, to reside in Chile.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment of USD 500,000 or equivalent. Residential real estate for personal use does not qualify. Eligible applicants include legal representatives, executives, and specialized technical/management personnel of qualifying foreign companies. Requires sponsorship letter from INVESTCHILE per Interior Ministry Supreme Decree No. 177 of 2022 (Arts. 62-64). Must apply from outside Chile. Eligible family members may apply as dependents.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Rentistas y Pensionados (Retired Persons and Income Earners)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals with sufficient regular passive income (pensions, rental income, dividends) to reside in Chile without engaging in employment.
visa_types.notes: Specific minimum income thresholds are not published on the official SERMIG site; third-party sources cite approximately USD 1,500-2,000/month but this could not be verified from official sources. Valid for up to 2 years, renewable. Path to permanent residency (Residencia Definitiva) after 24 months.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Reunificación Familiar (Family Reunification)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals with qualifying family bonds to Chilean citizens or permanent residents to reside in Chile.
visa_types.notes: Eligible relationships: spouses/civil union partners, parents, children under 18, children with disabilities (any age), unmarried students under 24, and minors under guardianship. Applications processed via SERMIG's Portal de Trámites Digitales with separate processes for applicants inside vs. outside Chile.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Tratamiento Médico (Medical Treatment)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals requiring medical treatment at Chilean health providers registered with the Superintendencia de Salud to reside in Chile for the duration of treatment.
visa_types.notes: Requires medical certificate detailing condition, treatment type, duration, and costs. Proof of economic means for subsistence and treatment financing required (health insurance acceptable). Extensions available with proof of ongoing treatment. Dependents may qualify. Must apply from outside Chile via SERMIG digital portal.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday - Australia
visa_types.purpose: Bilateral working holiday agreement allowing young Australian nationals to live and work temporarily in Chile.
visa_types.notes: Age limit: up to 31 years. Based on bilateral Working Holiday Agreement between Chile and Australia. Australia was also removed from DS 359 visa-required list effective Sep 2025, so Australians may now also enter visa-free as tourists for up to 90 days separately.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday - New Zealand
visa_types.purpose: Bilateral working holiday agreement allowing young New Zealand nationals to live and work temporarily in Chile.
visa_types.notes: Age limit: up to 30 years. Based on bilateral Working Holiday Agreement between Chile and New Zealand.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Acuerdos Internacionales / Mercosur (International Reciprocity)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residence based on bilateral or multilateral international treaties, including the Mercosur Residence Agreement for nationals of member and associate states.
visa_types.notes: Covers nationals of Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay under the Mercosur Residence Agreement (signed 6 Dec 2002, in force 28 Jul 2009). Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru ratified as of January 2024. Bolivia-Chile bilateral agreement eliminates application fees for Bolivian nationals. Holders may engage in lawful remunerated activities. Valid up to 2 years.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Personas Religiosas (Religious Persons)
visa_types.purpose: Allows members of recognized faiths and denominations to reside in Chile for religious activities.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 2 years, renewable. Must be members of recognized faiths and denominations. Part of the 15 Residencia Temporal subcategories under Ley N°21.325.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Razones Humanitarias (Humanitarian Reasons)
visa_types.purpose: Grants temporary residence to foreign nationals who are victims of violence or in vulnerable situations, including human trafficking victims, domestic violence victims, pregnant individuals, and children.
visa_types.notes: Five subcategories: children and adolescents, pregnant individuals, human trafficking victims, smuggling of migrant persons victims, and victims of domestic or gender-based violence. Duration and specific requirements vary by subcategory.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal - Gestión de Negocios (Business Management)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals conducting multi-entry commercial operations and business management activities in Chile to obtain temporary residence.
visa_types.notes: Distinct from the investor subcategory; covers those conducting commercial operations that require regular multi-entry presence. Valid for up to 2 years. Part of the 15 Residencia Temporal subcategories under Ley N°21.325.

visa_types.name: Permiso de Permanencia Transitoria - Residentes Oficiales que Terminan Misión (Official Residents Completing Mission)
visa_types.purpose: Covers foreign diplomatic and official residents completing their official mission in Chile, allowing continued temporary stay.
visa_types.notes: Part of the Permanencia Transitoria framework. Approximately 73 bilateral agreements exempt diplomatic/official/service passport holders from requiring a visa for stays up to 90 days per the MINREL VIDO Manual (May 2025).

visa_types.name: Residencia Definitiva (Permanent Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Grants the right to reside indefinitely in Chile and engage in any lawful activity without restrictions.
visa_types.notes: Standard eligibility: minimum 24 months of Residencia Temporal. Reduced to 12 months for those with family ties to Chilean nationals/permanent residents, prior official missions, sufficient income/pensions, active business investments, or international agreement qualifications. ID card renewed every 5 years. Absence for more than 2 continuous years voids the permit. Path to citizenship by naturalization after 5 total years of legal residence.

validation_notes: 2026-06-29: Second full re-verification pass against live official sources. No material changes required - all core data points confirmed accurate. Record is clean.

2026-06-29: Permanencia Transitoria 90-day stay limit re-confirmed on live SERMIG page (serviciomigraciones.cl/en/permanencia-transitoria-permit/). Single extension for further 90 days confirmed. Page last updated October 7, 2025.

2026-06-29: Indian US-visa/Green Card exemption re-verified against chile.gob.cl/nueva-delhi - verbatim text and conditions unchanged. Minimum 6 months visa validity, 90-day max stay, C-type transit visas excluded. Confirmed live.

2026-06-29: Dominican Republic US-visa exemption re-verified against chile.gob.cl/santo-domingo - verbatim text and conditions unchanged. Minimum 6 months validity, all US visa types except C (transit), tourist stay only. Confirmed live.

2026-06-29: Chinese US/Canadian visa exemption re-verified against chile.gob.cl/beijing - conditions unchanged. Both US and Canadian visa (all types except C-category), minimum 6 months remaining validity, 90-day tourist stay. Confirmed live.

2026-06-29: Investor residency USD 500,000 threshold re-confirmed on live SERMIG investors page - verbatim text states 'equal to or greater than five hundred thousand US dollars or its equivalent in other currencies'. Regulatory basis (Interior Ministry Supreme Decree No. 177 of 2022) confirmed.

2026-06-29: Residencia Definitiva 24-month standard / 12-month reduced timelines re-confirmed on live SERMIG Residencia Definitiva page. Last update noted December 16, 2025 - no procedural changes announced.

2026-06-29: No superseding 2026 decree found affecting nationality visa requirements. Decreto 256 (March 21, 2026) is an administrative HR matter (exempt position for SERMIG director), not a visa-policy change. DS 359 (published 17 Sep 2025) remains the operative 104-country visa-required list.

2026-06-29: consulado.gob.cl/informacion-sobre-visas-para-ingresar-a-chile confirmed as the live public-facing entry point for visa requirement lookups (dropdown-based per-country tool, no static list published); consistent with negation structure in visa_required.
2026-06-27: Full re-verification pass against live official sources. No material changes required - all core data points confirmed accurate.

Prior notes: Removed non-schema field 'visa_policy.requires_prior_authorization': the 104-country list was duplicated inside visa_policy under a non-canonical key. These nationalities are correctly and solely captured in visa_required.nationalities_iso3.
Prior notes: Added USD 46/day minimum funds requirement to visa_free notes and policy notes (confirmed from ChileAtiende Resolución Exenta Nº 3.464 de 2023).
Prior notes: DS 359 (published 17 Sep 2025) confirmed as the current operative decree for 104-country visa-required list; no superseding 2026 decree found.
Prior notes: All prior source_urls for conditional_access entries re-verified as live and accurate.
gaps: The visa-free list is presented as a negation of DS 359's 104-country list: individual visa-free nationality entries with specific stay-day confirmations per country are not enumerated on a single official page. The 90-day default is from the law and consulado.gob.cl.
Specific processing times for investor residency and working holiday visas not found on official SERMIG pages.
Exact minimum income threshold for Rentista/Pensionado category not confirmed verbatim from official SERMIG source (only third-party sources cite USD 1,500-2,000/month).
No official digital nomad visa exists; Chile has not launched a dedicated digital nomad visa program as of the research date.
Cuadro de Visas y Aranceles PDF (updated March 6, 2026) is binary-encoded and full country table could not be extracted; it supersedes the previous 2024 version.

Path from Mercosur residency to Residencia Definitiva is not explicitly stated on the official SERMIG subcategory page; the 2-year general rule is applied by inference from the broader Residencia Definitiva page.
Dual citizenship status for naturalized Chileans not confirmed from official source; omitted from CBI section accordingly.

Conditional US-visa exemption may apply to additional DS-359 nationalities beyond IND, DOM, CHN (e.g. Bangladesh, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Philippines) - this could not be confirmed from official consulate pages for each country; only IND, DOM, CHN were verified at official chile.gob.cl consulate sub-sites.

The full list of ~73 countries with diplomatic/official/service passport waivers is published only in the MINREL VIDO Manual (image-based PDF, May 2025 edition) which could not be machine-read. Individual agreements confirmed from official sources: Switzerland, Oman, Morocco, South Africa, Azerbaijan, Bolivia.

No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption found for Chile on any official source; OCI is an India-issued travel document for entry to India only and does not appear to confer Chile entry benefits.
Schengen visa holder exemption for Chile not found on any official source; aggregator sites sometimes cite this but it could not be verified from official Chilean government pages.
Dictamen N°D75/2026 (February 2026, Comptroller General's Office) appeared in search results but content not fully accessible; no evidence it changes visa-entry policy.

sources_checked.description: Decreto Supremo N°359 (signed 8 Nov 2024, published 17 Sep 2025) - Official list of 104 nationalities requiring prior authorization/visa to enter Chile as Permanencia Transitoria holders. Diario Oficial de la República de Chile.
SERMIG - Permanencia Transitoria permit overview, maximum 90-day stay, extension rules. Re-verified June 2026.
SERMIG - Investors and related personnel temporary residency permit. USD 500,000 threshold re-confirmed June 2026.
SERMIG - Mercosur international reciprocity residency subcategory.
SERMIG - Residencia Definitiva (permanent residency) official requirements. 24-month / 12-month timelines re-confirmed June 2026.
SERMIG - Full list of Residencia Temporal subcategories.
SERMIG - Immigration regulations overview. Confirmed no 2026 superseding decrees as of June 2026.
Consulado.gob.cl - Prior authorization or visa requirements page. Re-verified June 2026.
Consulado.gob.cl - Entry visa requirements page with dropdown per-country lookup tool. Re-verified June 2026.
Consulado.gob.cl - Visa and tariff table landing page (links to PDF updated March 6, 2026). Re-verified June 2026.
Consulado.gob.cl - Cuadro de Visas y Aranceles PDF (March 6, 2026 revision); binary-encoded, content not fully extractable.
ChileAtiende - Permanencia Transitoria ficha; confirms 90-day stay, USD 46/day minimum funds (Resolución Exenta Nº 3.464 de 2023).
ChileAtiende - Autorización previa o visa ficha; confirms Law 21.325 as governing basis. Re-verified June 2026.
Chile.gob.cl - Visa types for foreigners including Working Holiday agreements.
Embajada de Chile en Nueva Delhi - US visa/Green Card exemption for Indian nationals. Re-verified June 2026; conditions unchanged.
Consulado de Chile en Santo Domingo - US visa exemption for Dominican Republic nationals. Re-verified June 2026; conditions unchanged.
Sección Consular de Chile en Beijing - US or Canadian visa/Green Card exemption for Chinese nationals. Re-verified June 2026; conditions unchanged.
InvestChile blog - confirms USD 500,000 investor visa threshold, 2-year permit duration, path to permanent residency; SD 177/2022 regulatory basis.
InvestChile - Visas and foreign citizens overview page.

MINREL - Manual VIDO (Visas Diplomáticas y Oficiales), May 2025 edition - authoritative list of all ~73 countries with diplomatic passport visa exemption agreements with Chile; image-based PDF not machine-readable.
MINREL press release - Chile-Switzerland mutual visa exemption for diplomatic/official/service passport holders, signed 19 Jan 2024.
Chile en el Exterior (Egypt) - Chile-Oman mutual visa exemption for diplomatic/special/service/official passport holders, signed 22 Mar 2022.
Senado de Chile - Approval of diplomatic passport visa exemption agreements with Morocco (signed 20 Sep 2016) and South Africa (signed 1 Sep 2016).
MINREL press release - Chile-Azerbaijan visa exemption for diplomatic/service/official passport holders, signed 13 Sep 2018.
Cancillería de Bolivia - Bolivia-Chile diplomatic/official passport visa exemption agreement entered into force March 2026.
SERMIG - Residencia Temporal subcategory: Remunerated Activities (work permit), requirements and duration.
SERMIG - Residencia Temporal subcategory: Students, requirements and 30hr/week work authorization.
SERMIG - Residencia Temporal subcategory: Seasonal Workers, up to 5-year validity with 6-month/year stay limit.
SERMIG - Residencia Temporal subcategory: Family Reunification, eligible relationships and documentation.
SERMIG - Residencia Temporal subcategory: Medical Treatment, requirements including health insurance.
SERMIG - Residencia Temporal subcategory: Humanitarian Reasons, five subcategories including trafficking and domestic violence victims.
